summary.scope: Sprint 1 test generation covering EPIC-1 Core Task Platform (S-1.1 Auth, S-1.2 Task CRUD)
summary.testingApproach: Layered testing with unit, integration, and E2E coverage across API and web packages

testCases.name: should authenticate user with valid credentials
testCases.description: Verifies JWT token generation for valid email/password

testCases.name: should create task with required fields
testCases.description: Verifies task creation with title and projectId

testCases.name: should handle concurrent position updates
testCases.description: Tests drag-and-drop reordering under concurrent writes

testCases.name: should login and redirect to dashboard
testCases.description: Full browser login flow with redirect verification

testCases.name: should show error on OAuth failure
testCases.description: Verifies user-friendly error display on OAuth provider error

content.reportTitle: TaskFlow Pro - Sprint 1 Test Summary (EPIC-1 Core Platform)
content.executiveSummary: Sprint 1 focused on EPIC-1 Core Task Platform stories S-1.1 (Auth) and S-1.2 (Task CRUD). Overall test pass rate is 94.2% with 326 of 346 tests passing. 12 failures are minor UI regressions in edge cases, 8 are known flaky integration tests being investigated. No critical defects remain open. Code coverage stands at 83.7% for the api package and 78.2% for the web package.

testsByType.notes: 6 failures are in TaskCard snapshot tests due to design token updates not yet reflected in test fixtures
8 tests skipped due to flaky Redis connection in CI. Passes locally. Investigating CI Docker networking.
6 failures in login flow edge cases: password reset redirect, OAuth error handling, and session expiry. All have tickets filed.

stories.notes: Auth module well tested. Failures are edge cases in OAuth error handling and session timeout.
Task CRUD fully covered. 4 failures in drag-and-drop position recalculation with concurrent updates.

uncoveredAreas: Error recovery in integration sync jobs
Edge cases in date timezone conversion

defectsFound.title: Task drag-and-drop produces incorrect position when 2 users reorder simultaneously
OAuth error page shows raw error code instead of user-friendly message
Session expiry notification appears behind Focus Mode overlay

recommendations.recommendation: Fix snapshot test fixtures to match updated design tokens before Sprint 2
Investigate and resolve CI Redis connectivity flakiness for integration tests
Add retry logic to drag-and-drop position API for concurrent update scenarios
Increase E2E coverage for OAuth flows before EPIC-4 integration work begins

content.nextSprintTestPlan: Sprint 2 will cover S-1.3 (Project Organization) and S-2.1 (AI Priority Scoring). Key focus areas: project CRUD operations, label management, and initial AI model inference accuracy testing. Target: maintain >90% pass rate and >80% coverage.
